authoritative_sources.title: 6.0 Manage Customer Service Definitions and Key Measures [PCF 8.0]
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public service, support, client-delivery, or customer-workflow guidance relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: APQC is a recognized process and benchmarking authority, and its public customer-service process framework is authoritative for service workflow structure, request classification, and technical-service terminology.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: request intake, service classification, and support-workflow framing

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 10002:2018 Quality management - Customer satisfaction - Guidelines for complaints handling in organizations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO is the authoritative publisher of ISO 10002, the recognized complaint-handling standard used to structure complaint intake, response, escalation, closure, and evidence discipline for customer-facing cases.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: complaint intake, response, and closure control

authoritative_sources.title: 29 CFR 1910.1200 - Hazard Communication
authoritative_sources.source_class: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA's Hazard Communication standard is the primary U.S. rule for hazardous-chemical communication, SDS requirements, label alignment, and downstream-user information boundaries.

authoritative_sources.title: 29 CFR 1910.119 - Process Safety Management of Highly Hazardous Chemicals
authoritative_sources.source_class: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA's PSM standard is the primary U.S. threshold and process-safety authority for safe operating limits, process information, change control, and when technical-service questions must stop short of engineering or safety signoff.

authoritative_sources.title: Monitoring, Recordkeeping, and Reporting
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: EPA's Title V guidance page is the official U.S. permit-workflow reference for monitoring, recordkeeping, and reporting expectations that constrain troubleshooting, emissions-affecting trials, and field-guidance posture.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: permit-aware readiness and environmental evidence review

authoritative_sources.title: 40 CFR 70.6 - Permit content
authoritative_sources.source_class: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The eCFR permit-content rule defines required monitoring, recordkeeping, reporting, sampling, and inspection-entry conditions that technical service must treat as authoritative when a request touches site emissions or operating conditions.

authoritative_sources.title: Hazardous Waste Compliance Monitoring
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: EPA's hazardous-waste compliance-monitoring page is the official inspection workflow reference for record reviews, sampling inspections, and compliance evidence that constrain disposal or contaminated-material guidance.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: waste, incident, and inspection evidence review

authoritative_sources.title: ISO/TC 251 Asset Management Overview
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO/TC 251 is the official ISO source for ISO 55001 and related asset-management standards, making it a recognized authority for asset condition, lifecycle evidence, maintenance dependencies, and field-evidence posture.

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and minimum-facts grounding
workflow_stages.description: Capture the customer request, product and lot identifiers, site and jurisdiction context, claimed failure mode, and tenant policy posture before attempting resolution.
source_classes: Public service, support, client-delivery, or customer-workflow guidance relevant to the lane
Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards

workflow_stages.name: Product-data, lot, and complaint evidence review
workflow_stages.description: Review SDS, TDS, COA, approved application guidance, complaint history, lot genealogy, retain-sample results, and CAPA status to determine what the record actually supports.
source_classes: Public service, support, client-delivery, or customer-workflow guidance relevant to the lane
Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards

workflow_stages.name: Field-condition, asset, and calibration dependency check
workflow_stages.description: Assess field notes, equipment settings, maintenance records, calibration certificates, and operating conditions to determine whether the issue belongs in technical service or another lane.
source_classes: Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards
Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance

workflow_stages.name: Permit, inspection, and regulated evidence handling
workflow_stages.description: Verify whether troubleshooting or trial guidance touches permits, emissions, waste handling, monitoring, sampling, or inspection obligations that require documented evidence before the lane can respond safely.
source_classes: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Resolution and stakeholder communication packaging
workflow_stages.description: Prepare evidence-backed resolution notes and customer or stakeholder communications that explain what is known, what action was taken, and what remains unresolved without overstating authority.
source_classes: Public service, support, client-delivery, or customer-workflow guidance relevant to the lane
Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards

workflow_stages.name: Exception, repeat-failure, and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate unsupported exceptions, repeated failures, field-risk changes, source conflicts, and requests for engineering, EHS, or regulatory signoff with the required evidence package and next owner.
source_classes: Public service, support, client-delivery, or customer-workflow guidance relevant to the lane
Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references

artifacts: customer request records, case notes, service-level commitments, and communication logs
product identifiers, grade and lot records, batch genealogy, formulation status, and document-control revisions
SDS, TDS, COA, COC, approved application guidance, technical bulletins, and product-change notices
complaint narratives, sample-chain records, retain-sample tests, lab results, CAPA or 8D records, and repeat-failure history
field trial plans, process conditions, operator notes, photographs, safety observations, and incident logs
permit conditions, sampling results, inspection findings, monitoring logs, and waste-handling records
asset condition records, calibration certificates, work orders, maintenance notes, and instrument status reports
resolution notes, communication summaries, escalation packages, and request-resolution summaries
systems_of_record: QMS or complaint and CAPA platform

allowed: Classify requests as in scope, out of scope, blocked, complaint-driven, exception-driven, or owner-transfer sensitive.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Provide evidence-backed product-data explanations, troubleshooting posture, next-step summaries, and stakeholder communications without overstating authority.
Prepare escalation packages and handoff summaries when ownership shifts to EHS, maintenance, engineering, regulatory, or other specialist owners.
escalate: Required SDS, TDS, COA, lot, complaint, permit, inspection, sampling, maintenance, calibration, or incident records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires engineering, environmental, safety, regulatory-reporting, disposal, legal, or commercial approval authority.
Field conditions, odor or emissions observations, waste implications, or threshold-sensitive process changes materially alter the risk posture.
Public baseline guidance and tenant policy or customer-specific commitments materially conflict.
refuse: Approve off-spec use, off-label applications, unsupported workarounds, or process changes beyond documented authority.
Sign off on engineering, environmental, safety, permit, disposal, or regulatory judgments outside lane scope.
Promise product suitability, complaint outcome, or compliant operation when the authoritative record does not support it.

domain_failure_modes: The lane gives customer-facing technical guidance from partial facts and turns a blocked case into an unsafe recommendation.
A customer-site equipment, calibration, or maintenance problem is misdiagnosed as a product issue because field evidence was ignored.
Troubleshooting advice touches emissions, waste, or permit-controlled operations without confirming monitoring, sampling, or inspection evidence.
The lane approves off-spec, off-label, or workaround behavior that should have remained with quality, product stewardship, EHS, or engineering authority.
Repeat failure complaints are treated as routine cases instead of being escalated with CAPA and ownership visibility.
Conflicting SDS, permit, field, or tenant-policy sources are smoothed over into a confident answer instead of escalated.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Permit, threshold, or inspection evidence gap
Field condition that changes the risk posture
Request for engineering, safety, or authority-side signoff
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Customer-site permit obligations, waste determinations, and process-safety threshold applicability vary by jurisdiction, facility, and product use case.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: This pack uses OSHA and EPA sources as the public baseline. Runtime must retrieve the tenant's site, jurisdiction, permit scope, threshold applicability, and approval-owner map before resolving any request that touches emissions, waste, or safe-operating claims.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant commercial concession policies, product-family restrictions, and customer-specific technical-service SLAs can materially narrow what technical service may promise.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The public baseline does not authorize credits, off-spec releases, or bespoke product claims. Tenant policy retrieval is required before any exception-sensitive communication is finalized.

notes: The research baseline covers workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure behavior instead of only generic customer-service scripting.

The lane stops at evidence-backed technical-service resolution and communication. Product redesign, engineering signoff, EHS judgment, waste classification, permit interpretation, regulatory reporting, and commercial exception approval remain outside the lane.
